back_translation: King Nebuchadnezzar made a golden statue sixty cubits high and six cubits wide, and he set it up on the plain of Dura in the province of Babylon.

Then King Nebuchadnezzar sent word to summon the satraps, prefects, governors, advisers, treasurers, judges, magistrates, and all the officials of the provinces to come to the dedication of the statue he had set up.

So the satraps, prefects, governors, advisers, treasurers, judges, magistrates, and all the officials of the provinces came to the dedication of the statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up, and they stood before it.
Then the herald proclaimed loudly, 'O peoples of every nation and language, you are commanded as follows:
When you hear the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipes, and all kinds of music, you must fall down and worship the golden statue that King Nebuchadnezzar has set up.
Whoever does not fall down and worship will immediately be thrown into the blazing fiery furnace.'

Therefore, as soon as all the people heard the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, and all kinds of music, the people of every nation and language fell down and worshiped the golden statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up.
At that time some Chaldeans came forward and maliciously accused the Jews.
They said to King Nebuchadnezzar, 'O king, may you live forever!
O king, you have issued a decree that everyone who hears the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipes, and all kinds of music must fall down and worship the golden statue,
and whoever does not fall down and worship will be thrown into the blazing fiery furnace.

There are some Jews you appointed to manage the affairs of the province of Babylon—Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego—who have not obeyed you, O king; they do not serve your gods and do not worship the golden statue you set up.'
Then Nebuchadnezzar, furious and enraged, commanded that Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego be brought in. So these men were brought before the king.
Nebuchadnezzar said to them, 'Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, is it true that you do not serve my gods and do not worship the golden statue I set up?

Now if you are ready, when you hear the sound of all the music, to fall down and worship the statue I made, well and good. But if you do not worship, you will be thrown at once into the blazing fiery furnace. Then what god will deliver you from my hand?'
Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego answered the king, 'O Nebuchadnezzar, we have no need to answer you in this matter.
If the God whom we serve exists, He is able to deliver us from the blazing fiery furnace and from your hand, O king.
But even if He does not, let it be known to you, O king, that we will not serve your gods or worship the golden statue you set up.'
Then Nebuchadnezzar was filled with rage, and the expression of his face changed toward Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. He ordered the furnace heated seven times hotter than usual,
and he commanded the strongest soldiers in his army to bind Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego and throw them into the blazing fiery furnace.
So these men were bound, still wearing their robes, trousers, turbans, and other garments, and were thrown into the blazing fiery furnace.
Because the king's command was so urgent and the furnace so hot, the flames killed the men who carried Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego up.
And these three men, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, fell into the blazing fiery furnace still bound.
Then King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished and rose up in haste. He asked his advisers, 'Did we not throw three men, bound, into the fire?' They answered the king, 'Certainly, O king.'
The king said, 'Look! I see four men walking around in the fire, unbound and unharmed, and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods.'

Then Nebuchadnezzar approached the door of the blazing fiery furnace and said, 'Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, servants of the Most High God, come out!' So Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego came out of the fire.

When the satraps, prefects, governors, and the king's advisers gathered, they saw that the fire had no power over the bodies of these men; the hair of their heads was not singed, their robes were unchanged, and there was no smell of fire on them.

Nebuchadnezzar said, 'Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, who sent His angel to deliver His servants who trusted in Him. They defied the king's command and gave up their lives rather than serve or worship any god except their own God.

Therefore I issue a decree that any nation or language that speaks blasphemy against the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego will be cut into pieces and their houses made a heap of rubbish, for there is no other god who can deliver in this way.'
Then the king promoted Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the province of Babylon.
King Nebuchadnezzar, to all the peoples and languages who dwell in all the earth: May your prosperity be multiplied. (MT 3:31 = English/BSB 4:1)
I am pleased to declare the signs and wonders that the Most High God has done for me. (MT 3:32 = English/BSB 4:2)
How great are His signs, and how mighty His wonders! His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and His dominion endures from generation to generation. (MT 3:33 = English/BSB 4:3)
